body: Bead reasoning was sound — the worker's intake just flagged acceptance criteria changes during pre-claim due to a noisy worktree (stale .ddx/executions files). That root cause is now resolved (the cleanup committed as 9d77b81a). Bead description and acceptance criteria are still valid; retry the implementation. Recent live incident on 2026-05-11 confirmed the value: vidar oMLX endpoint went down and 70 cells silently failed before detection — exactly what this preflight check would have caught in <30s.
Operator reviewed intake block; retry unchanged to keep worker queue moving.
Operator normalized acceptance into explicit numbered criteria from the existing description; retry with corrected acceptance.
Operator corrected acceptance and restarted worker with stronger pre-claim model after repeated gpt-5.4-mini schema failures; retry.
Operator disabled intake rewrites in bead-lifecycle skill and acceptance is already explicit; retry.
Operator patched local DDx pre-claim intake to stop requesting rewrites; retry.

[{"ac":1,"text":"With sindri-llamacpp configured to use `reasoning: low`, `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp,vidar --dry-run` exits non-zero in under 60s with a `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED` message naming the unsupported reasoning parameter and does not print any per-lane \"starting\" marker.","kind":"negative","verifiable":true},{"ac":2,"text":"With sindri-llamacpp correctly configured, the same command prints `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp OK (1 token in \u003cwall\u003es)` and continues to the lane plan output normally.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":3,"text":"With the llama-server process stopped, `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: connection refused` or curl error 7, and no matrix runner is spawned.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":4,"text":"`scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes openrouter-qwen36 --dry-run` does not call OpenRouter and prints `[preflight] openrouter-qwen36 SKIPPED (managed cloud lane)`.","kind":"negative","verifiable":true},{"ac":5,"text":"`scripts/benchmark/test_preflight_lane.bash` or an equivalent shellspec test covers the misconfigured local lane, correctly configured local lane, endpoint-down local lane, and managed-cloud skip cases against a mock HTTP server.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false}]
Operator made ACs mechanically verifiable and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata; retry.
